Safety Apr 2, 2026

Investigators: LaGuardia Controller May Have Stepped Away Before Deadly Crash

The incident quickly turned tragic.

Operational fatigue and staffing gaps at major airports may create dangerous single-point-of-failure scenarios where critical control positions lack adequate coverage.

Commercial Apr 2, 2026

The Real Reason Why Rolls-Royce Owns The Airbus A350 Engine Slot

A closer look at the host of advancements offered by the Trent XWB, and how it continues to develop.

Rolls-Royce's dominance in A350 powerplants stems from the Trent XWB's continuous evolution in fuel efficiency and modular design, creating high switching costs for operators.

See Simple Flying →

Air Cargo Apr 2, 2026

5 Reasons Why Airlines Still Fly The Boeing 747 Freighter

The Boeing 747 freighter remains valuable for its payload, cargo volume, nose-loading capability, and long-haul performance.

Despite newer aircraft availability, the 747F's unique nose-loading door and unmatched cargo volume make it irreplaceable for oversized cargo that cannot fit in modern narrowbody freighters.

See Simple Flying →

Former Sierra Space CEO Tom Vice to lead Astrion

Huntsville-based defense contractor focuses on systems engineering and integration, and space mission assurance...

Vice's appointment signals Astrion's strategic pivot toward higher-tier space systems integration work, leveraging Sierra Space's commercial spaceflight expertise to compete in the defense-space sector.

Military Apr 2, 2026

Pentagon considers installing anti-drone lasers at sensitive US sites

The Pentagon is moving toward wider use of laser-based counter-drone systems at select US locations as concerns mount…...

Laser counter-drone systems represent a shift toward directed-energy weapons for airspace protection, requiring aviation stakeholders to understand how these systems may affect flight operations near sensitive installations.

Safety Apr 2, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation safety culture from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating risks before incidents happen.

FAA Releases Part 141 Modernization Proposal

Report outlines proposals to alter certificated flight school approval, oversight and training technology.

The FAA's modernization proposal could reduce flight school certification timelines and enable adoption of advanced training technologies like simulation and AI-based instruction systems.

Safety Apr 2, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure requires strict procedural compliance for RNO runway 17 operations due to terrain and airspace constraints that demand heightened pilot situational awareness.

The Airbus A350 cabin is 5 inches wider than the Boeing 787—here’s how that small difference transforms seat comfort and long-haul travel.

A 5-inch cabin width advantage translates to meaningful revenue optimization for airlines through premium seat configurations and competitive differentiation on ultra-long-haul routes.

Aerospace Apr 2, 2026

Artemis II Astronauts Launch to Moon

NASA’s Space Launch System rocket and Orion spacecraft lift off in this April 1, 2026, image. NASA’s Artemis II mission will take NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy on an approximately 10-day journey around the Moon and...

Artemis II's crewed lunar orbit mission validates SLS-Orion infrastructure for sustained deep-space exploration, directly enabling future surface landings and commercial lunar logistics partnerships.

Regulatory Apr 2, 2026

Washington Repeals Aircraft Tax Before It Takes Effect

Measure enacted in 2025 is replaced with fuel tax and fee increases.

Washington's shift from aircraft purchase tax to fuel and fee increases transfers burden from capital expenditure to operational costs, affecting fleet acquisition economics differently than the original measure.

Global Combat Air Programme Awards First Contract with Edgewing

The first joint international contract has been awarded for the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), focusing on key design and engineering activities. The GCAP International Government Organisation (GIGO), which represents the governments of the UK, Italy and Japan, awarded a £686 million (about...

The UK, Italy, and Japan's first joint GCAP contract signals a new model for cost-sharing and technology collaboration in developing next-generation fighter aircraft.

Regional geopolitical escalation is forcing airlines to recalculate avionics costs and insurance premiums that had previously underestimated conflict-zone operational risks.

Semiconductor-based avionics are replacing traditional hardware architectures, signaling a fundamental shift in how airlines maintain and upgrade aircraft systems at scale.

Safety Apr 2, 2026

Climate Change Is Rewriting the Rules of Turbulence—and Cockpits Are Playing Catch-Up

Global Avionics Round-Up from Aircraft Value News (AVN) For decades, turbulence was treated as a known nuisance—unpredictable, occasionally dangerous, but broadly manageable within the design assumptions of modern aircraft. Engineers […]...

Climate-driven turbulence patterns are increasingly exceeding historical design parameters that aircraft and detection systems were engineered around, forcing modernization of avionics and operational procedures.

See Aviation Today →

Industry Apr 2, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Integrated optimization across airline, airport, and flight operations—rather than siloed improvements—offers the most promising path to simultaneously accommodate growing demand while reducing aviation's environmental footprint.

See MDPI →

Military Apr 2, 2026

Army’s SkyFoundry Plan To Build Own Cheap Drones Has ‘Evolved,’ Official Notes Commercial Involvement

The Army’s SkyFoundry initiative to manufacture thousands of its own cheap drones on a monthly basis has recently “evolved,” with an official telling sister publication Defense Daily the program is “100 […]...

The Army's shift toward commercial partnerships in SkyFoundry signals a strategic pivot from organic manufacturing to leverage existing supply chains and reduce development costs.

Autonomous flight integration between Northrop Grumman and Shield AI demonstrates practical progress toward military unmanned systems capability requirements.

Military Apr 2, 2026

Army Accepts First Optionally-Piloted Black Hawk, Will Inform Scaling Autonomy Across The Fleet

The Army announced Friday it has officially accepted the first Black Hawk helicopter outfitted to fly autonomously, with the plans for the aircraft to now undergo a “rigorous testing phase.” […]...

The Army's autonomous Black Hawk acceptance marks a critical validation point that will guide full-fleet autonomy integration timelines and operational doctrine across military rotorcraft.
